region_of_origin: Iran (Persia); widely adopted across the Indian subcontinent
food_subgroup: Cold dessert drink / layered dessert beverage

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn URI exists for Falooda as a specific named dish. Mapped to the broader 'dessert food product' class as the closest defensible FoodOn anchor.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not contain a specific code for Falooda. A0CKP (milk-based dessert) is used as the closest defensible FoodEx2 ancestor given the primary milk and ice cream components. No exact FoodEx2 code exists for this composite assembled dessert drink.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for Falooda as a specific food item.

serving.natural_language_portion: A standard serving of Falooda is one tall glass of approximately 300 ml (300 g), providing around 420 kcal, 66 g carbohydrates, 7.2 g protein, and 14.4 g fat.

nutrient_source.title: Nutritive Value of Indian Foods
nutrient_source.organization: Indian Council of Medical Research – National Institute of Nutrition (ICMR-NIN), India
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single ICMR-NIN entry exists for Falooda as a composite dish. Nutrient values are estimated from ICMR-NIN component data for full-cream milk, cornstarch vermicelli, rose syrup, basil seeds, and vanilla ice cream. Values cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for individual components (milk, cornstarch, ice cream, nuts). The composite per-100g profile reflects a typical assembled Falooda with approximately 40% milk, 20% ice cream, 15% rose syrup, 15% falooda sev, 5% sabja seeds, and 5% garnish.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — analogous estimate based on sweetened milk-based dessert with refined sugar and cornstarch
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS et al., Diabetes Care 2021
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Falooda in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 75 is an analogous estimate based on: (1) sweetened condensed milk desserts (GI ~61–80), (2) cornstarch-based products (GI ~67–85), and (3) rose syrup (predominantly sucrose, GI ~65). The composite high-sugar, high-refined-starch profile supports a GI estimate of approximately 75 (high). Glycemic load per 300 g serving = (75 × 66 g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 49.5 g, classified as very high GL.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium but high saturated fat and caloric density from full-cream milk and ice cream
hypertension.guidance: Falooda is naturally low in sodium (35 mg per 100 g) and provides potassium (110 mg) and calcium (85 mg) from milk, which are beneficial for blood pressure regulation; however, the high saturated fat content (~2.9 g per 100 g) and caloric density of a full 300 g serving (~420 kcal) warrant portion control for individuals managing hypertension with weight or lipid targets.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very high sugar content (18 g per 100 g), high GI (~75), and very high glycemic load per serving (~49.5)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Falooda is not recommended for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes due to its very high free sugar content from rose syrup and ice cream, a high glycemic index of approximately 75, and a glycemic load of ~49.5 per standard 300 g serving, all of which will cause rapid and significant postprandial blood glucose elevation; sugar-free syrup and low-fat frozen yogurt substitutions may reduce but not eliminate this risk.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from full-cream milk and ice cream, high added sugar, and high caloric density
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Regular consumption of Falooda poses a moderate cardiovascular risk due to its saturated fat content (~2.9 g per 100 g, ~8.7 g per serving) from full-cream milk and ice cream, combined with high added sugar (~54 g per serving), which together may adversely affect LDL cholesterol and triglyceride levels; occasional consumption in small portions is preferable for individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High refined sugar and high-GI carbohydrates may promote systemic inflammation
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should limit Falooda consumption as its high refined sugar content and high glycemic index may exacerbate systemic inflammation and worsen joint symptoms.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Falooda is a celebrated Indian cold dessert drink with deep roots in Persian culinary tradition, widely enjoyed across the Indian subcontinent and among South Asian diaspora communities globally. It is served in a tall glass as a visually striking, multi-layered concoction that combines contrasting textures and flavors. The assembly begins with sweet rose syrup at the base, followed by sabja seeds (sweet basil seeds, also called tukmaria or tukmalanga) that have been soaked in water until they develop a gelatinous, tapioca-like coating. These are layered with falooda sev — delicate, translucent vermicelli noodles made from cornstarch — and chilled full-cream milk. The dessert is crowned with a scoop of rich vanilla ice cream or kulfi (a dense, slow-churned Indian frozen dairy dessert), and garnished with slivered pistachios and almonds. The result is a drink-dessert hybrid that is simultaneously cold, creamy, sweet, and subtly floral. Nutritionally, a standard 300 g serving provides approximately 420 kcal, 22 g carbohydrates per 100 g (predominantly from added sugars at 18 g per 100 g), 4.8 g fat, and 2.4 g protein. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 75 (high), making it unsuitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes or those following low-sugar diets. It is a good source of calcium (85 mg per 100 g) and vitamin B12 (0.4 mcg per 100 g) from the dairy components. Falooda is a quintessential summertime treat and a popular choice for Iftar during Ramadan. It is vegetarian and halal-friendly but contains dairy and tree nuts.
common_user_queries: What are sabja seeds in falooda?
What is falooda sev made of?
Can I eat falooda during Ramadan?
What is the difference between falooda and kulfi falooda?

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for Falooda as a composite dish. Nutrient values are estimated from ICMR-NIN component data for full-cream milk, cornstarch vermicelli, rose syrup, basil seeds, and vanilla ice cream, cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy. The GI value is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database framework applied to comparable high-sugar milk-based desserts. Confidence is medium rather than low because the component-level data from ICMR-NIN and USDA are well-established, and the composite proportions reflect a standard Falooda recipe.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are composite estimates derived from ICMR-NIN and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy component data; no single approved-source entry exists for Falooda as a named dish. The assumed composition is approximately 40% full-cream milk, 20% vanilla ice cream, 15% rose syrup, 15% falooda sev (cornstarch vermicelli), 5% sabja seeds, and 5% nut garnish. 2. water_g is set to -1 as detailed moisture data for the assembled composite Falooda is not available in ICMR-NIN or USDA FoodData Central as of 2024. 3. GI of 75 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for Falooda exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. 4. The input record listed duplicate portions (four identical entries of 300 g); deduplicated to a single default serving of 300 g. 5. The input dietary_tags included 'low_calorie' and 'low_fat', which are inaccurate for a 140 kcal/100 g, 4.8 g fat/100 g product yielding ~420 kcal per serving; these tags have been removed. 6. Saturated fat (2.9 g), monounsaturated_fat_g (1.3 g), polyunsaturated_fat_g (0.2 g), trans_fat_g (0.0 g), and cholesterol_mg (18 mg) per 100 g are estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for whole milk and vanilla ice cream components. 7. Falooda is a Persian-influenced Indian dish; multilingual naming requirements follow the Pan-Indian origin category. Hindi and Tamil names are provided per input aliases. No Chinese, Malay, Japanese, Korean, Indonesian, Thai, or Vietnamese names are applicable for this dish. 8. ontology.foodex2_match_type corrected from 'analogous_estimate' to 'closest_parent' (the only allowed values are 'exact', 'closest_parent', and 'none'); the foodex2_gap_note documents the estimation basis in full.

Iteration 2: Corrected ontology.foodon_match_type from 'broader' to 'closest_parent'. Attempted to set foodex2_match_type to 'analogous_estimate' — this was rejected by validator as not an allowed value.
